macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Stock Market Volatile on Iran Tensions, Closes Near Flat
items.summary: US stocks swung from sharp early losses to minimal gains on Monday as geopolitical tensions with Iran drove oil prices up 6% but past military conflicts suggested limited sustained market impact. The S&P 500 rose less than 0.1% to 6,881.62, while the Nasdaq composite gained 0.4% and the Dow fell 0.1%.

items.title: Oil Surges on Iran Strikes; S&P 500 Holds Below 6,900 Pivot
items.summary: Following US and Israel attacks on Iran over the weekend, crude prices jumped over 6% on concerns about disrupted global oil supply and inflation. The S&P 500 ended up 0.04% but remained below the critical 6,900 technical level, with potential downside targets at 6,730 if support breaks.

items.title: Airlines and Cruise Lines Lead Market Declines Amid Fuel Cost Concerns
items.summary: Transportation stocks suffered significant losses as higher oil prices threaten already substantial fuel bills, while Middle East airport closures stranded travelers. Oil and defense stocks rallied in response to geopolitical developments, with Nvidia rising 2.9% to help support the broader market recovery.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: $1 Billion Inflows into Crypto Funds Reverse Five-Week $4B Outflow Streak
items.summary: Crypto investment products saw $1 billion in net inflows last week, ending a five-week period of $4 billion outflows, driven by price softness and renewed buying from major Bitcoin holders. Bitcoin and Ethereum experienced volatility from US strikes on Iran, dipping to $63,000 and below $2,000 before recovering, with Solana leading altcoin inflows at $53.8 million.[1]

items.title: Bitcoin Enters Fair Value Zone Amid Subdued Selling After Iran Strikes
items.summary: Despite US strikes on Iran causing initial price dips, no panic selling occurred in crypto markets, with Bitcoin entering a fair value zone per MVRV Z-Score at 0.39, signaling potential rebound. Short-term holder inflows to exchanges remain low, indicating weak hands already exited.[2]

items.title: Bitmine Accumulates Ethereum as Crypto Bottom Approaches
items.summary: Bitmine has been buying about $100 million in Ethereum weekly, building a cash balance over $800 million, positioning for a major purchase at the market bottom. This accumulation aligns with bullish indicators like Bitcoin's fair value entry and lack of panic selling.[2]

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Keysight and Samsung Demonstrate End-to-End AI-RAN Validation Workflow
items.summary: Keysight and Samsung showcased a joint demonstration at MWC 2026 that streamlines data collection, AI and machine learning training, and benchmarking for AI-driven radio access networks.

items.title: UTA Researcher Uses AI to Challenge Brain-Navigation Link
items.summary: Steven Weisberg at The University of Texas at Arlington found that advanced AI tools, including deep convolutional neural networks, could not detect a clear link between brain structure and navigation ability in healthy young adults.

items.title: CEOs Rank AI as Top Business Risk Surpassing Geopolitics and Cyber Threats
items.summary: For the first time, CEOs identify artificial intelligence as the biggest business risk, ahead of geopolitical turmoil, cyber intrusions, and economic instability, according to The Conference Board survey.

items.title: UK Government Launches Call for Evidence on AI Data in Energy Systems
items.summary: The UK government issued a call for evidence on energy datasets for AI applications to enhance grid operations, renewable forecasting, and decarbonisation efforts like heat pumps and solar output prediction.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: World of Warcraft: Midnight Expansion Launches
items.summary: Blizzard's 11th major expansion for World of Warcraft releases today on PC, introducing new regions, endgame activities, and player housing systems. The expansion is expected to drive significant subscription increases and reassert the franchise's dominance in the PC live-service space.

items.title: Xbox Game Pass Adds Kingdom Come: Deliverance 2 and Final Fantasy 3
items.summary: Two major titles join Xbox Game Pass today as part of March's wave 2 announcement. Kingdom Come: Deliverance 2 and Final Fantasy 3 are now available on Ultimate, Premium, and PC tiers.

items.title: Legacy of Kain: Defiance Remastered Releases Today
items.summary: The modernized remaster of the cult-favorite 2003 action-adventure launches today with upgraded visuals and refined controls, targeting both longtime fans and newcomers to the gothic franchise.

items.title: Nintendo Indie World Showcase Scheduled for Today
items.summary: Nintendo announces a 15-minute Indie World Showcase today focusing on indie games for Nintendo Switch and Switch 2. The presentation is expected to feature enhanced editions of current hits and new titles utilizing Switch 2's enhanced capabilities.

items.title: Slay the Spire 2 Enters Early Access on March 5
items.summary: The sequel to the influential deck-building roguelike launches in Early Access with new characters, expanded systems, and deeper mechanical complexity, expected to resonate with strategy players and mid-core audiences.

items.title: Bungie's Marathon Extraction Shooter Launches March 5
items.summary: Bungie re-enters the competitive gaming arena with Marathon, a science-fiction extraction shooter designed for long-term player retention through tight gunplay and seasonal content.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Cisco Reserves Right to Cancel Compute Orders and Adjust Pricing; Lenovo Warns of March Price Hikes
items.summary: Cisco has updated its policy to allow cancellation of compute orders and pricing adjustments between order and ship dates. Lenovo is warning of impending price increases in March, impacting enterprise hardware quoting strategies.

items.title: ZenaTech Wins 2026 'Most Prolific and Proficient Acquirer' Award for Drone as a Service Expansion
items.summary: ZenaTech received the award from Morrissey Goodale for acquiring companies to build a global Drone as a Service network, integrating drones for inspection, monitoring, and other sectors.

items.title: Samsung Unveils Galaxy S26 Series and Galaxy Z TriFold at MWC 2026 with New Privacy and Security Features
items.summary: At MWC 2026, Samsung showcased the Galaxy S26 series with Privacy Display on the Ultra model, Galaxy Z TriFold's 10-inch multi-folding display, and security enhancements like Knox Vault and Knox Matrix.

items.title: WD Announces Entire 2026 Hard Drive Production Sold Out
items.summary: Western Digital reports that all hard drive production for 2026 is already allocated, signaling tight supply amid rising demand.
